headline: Europe seeks to defuse Trump criticism ahead of NATO summit in Ankara
description: Disoriented by a flurry of announcements regarding the stationing of US troops on the continent, European foreign ministers sought to convince Marco Rubio of their efforts to take greater responsibility for collective defense and to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

description: A former influencer known as 'PA7' and a counterfeiter known as 'the Prince of Fake Luxury Watches' are suspected of having illegally imported hundreds of kilograms of 'Cali' marijuana.

headline: Paris left splits over awarding of city's marathon to Bolloré-linked company
description: The Cadence group – which notably includes Havas, owned by billionaire Vincent Bolloré – will organize the next four Paris marathons and half-marathons, the Paris City Council decided on Wednesday.

headline: Cannes: 'Everytime' by Sandra Wollner picks up the Un Certain Regard Prize
description: The competition of 18 feature films was marked by stories exploring the maternal bond and the disappearance of young girls.

headline: Cubans on the specter of US intervention: 'On what grounds would a foreign government have the right to decide for us?'
description: In Havana, the possibility of a US intervention has once again become a topic of conversation. Cuba's civil defense has shared a guide explaining how families can protect themselves in case of a 'military attack.'

description: France attracted the most foreign investment in Europe in 2025, according to the EY barometer published on Thursday, despite an overall decline in the number of projects and associated jobs across the continent.

headline: Who has been studying France's elites since Pierre Bourdieu, and what are they learning?
description: The rise in global inequality has rekindled researchers' interest in the world of the upper bourgeoisie. At the intersection of economic and social issues, their work is yielding valuable insights into the mechanisms that perpetuate a certain social order.

headline: Pakistan's powerful military leader Asim Munir is reshaping the country
description: In the year since Pakistan's brief conflict with India in May 2025, the head of Pakistan's armed forces has used the war to consolidate his hold on the state, further erasing the distinction between civilian and military power.
